Christopher Veau 07/07/2022 3.0 Adding DB trigger to adjust non-cig locations with negative on-hand qty to 0 Christopher Veau 08/01/2022 4.0 Removing DB trigger, transactions updated to adjust locations picked to negative on-hand EX3 5 – User Short Auto-Adjust Inventory Mc Lane chases inventory throughout the day at various points to ensure that everything that they can pick for that day is picked and shipped . Current state, to adjust inventory of a pick location, a cycle counter must count and book the count. To expedite this process, McLane has approved picking teammates to make an immediate adjustment to the inventory with a user short during picking. For this reason, WMOS is customized to create an inventory adjustment and an associated PIX that is triggered when a floor user executes a user short. With this change, the next chase wave that is run, which could be run immediately after the short, create s a replenishment there is inventory available to replenish the shorted location. Additionally, once inventory reaches zero and or shorted using ctrl-d with negative on e hand quantity, EX28 Replenishment Task Release and Priority Bumping is able to release and/or bump replenishments to reach the location quicker. To raise the chances that the user is shorting the correct Pick Location, WMOS is customized to prompt for a Location Confirmation Scan after the user initiates short in a full short scenario. McLane is also wanting locations which have been driven to negative on-hand inventory by completion of a pick (without shorting) to be adjusted up to have an on-hand qty of 0. The main reason is to not have over replenishments created for locations due to the negative inventory. Currently this is happening since the replenishments take into account the negative quantity. McLane chooses which location this is to be applied to. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Pack Cubed Directed – N ew Update Functionality to Zero Out Location Inventory if a User Shorts a Pick Reporting N Other N Mc Lane picks RF Full Case Picking and RF Active Picking allows the users to short during picking. This short generates a Picking Short Item record which can be chased. Since McLane is constantly running chase throughout the day, it ensures that replenishment can be created at the next chase opportunity if the inventory is immediately adjuste d at the time of the short. For this reason, RF Pack Cubed Directed transaction is modified to automatically adjust the on-hand inventory of the Pick Location to 0 if the user has shorted a pick. Additionally, WMOS sends a PIX to the host system alerting the change in inventory levels. There will be no changes to base cycle count generation or behavior as part of this extension. McLane team decides if they want to continue generating the Cycle Counts, Recounts, Booking, and Immediate or Deferred Update Configuration. Additionally, RF Pack Cubed Directed and RF Pack Pick Cart are customized to prompt for a Location Scan to confirm the Location in a full short scenario. For the locations which have been driven to negative on-hand inventory by completion of a pick (without shorting) a DB trigger MCLN_TUB_WMI_NEGATIVE is used to set these locations to a on-hand qty of 0. The base system code B-331 is leveraged to indicate which pick location determination zones this trigger is applied to. Assumptions This transaction modifies shorting updates in two transactions: RF Pack Cubed Directed RF Pack Pick Cart Transactions are either enabled for this behavior or disabled for this behavior and that is the only check . EX35 sends a PIX to host notifying the inventory adjustment with whichever reason code is configured in the custom system code. There are no changes to base generation and execution of Cycle Counts in this extension. There are no changes to base short warning messages as part of this extension. This extension does not change base allocation trigger points. Updating the on-hand inventory will not create a replenishment allocation. Existing pick allocations from the shorted location will be unchanged. EX35 is not utilized with Cigarette Picking. EX35 ensures that there are currently no replenishment tasks in released/in progress/Locked/Disabled status before generating an allocation for a new replenishment task. Dependencies Code Dependencies EX 12 – Full Case Picking Changes . This extension must function with EX12 Active Picking shorting. EX33 – RF Picking for Active. This extension must function with EX33 EX28 – Replenishment Task Release and Priority Bumping. EX28 works to release tasks after EX35 has zeroed out inventory in the pick locations if possible. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension . First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 35 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 35 is turned off, and no custom EX 35 processing will occur. Lastly, RF Pack Cubed Directed Transaction Parameter 508-508 must be enabled. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues with custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension control for EX35 section 6.1 logic is turned off . Section 6.1.2 behavior is enabled if RF Pack Cubed Directed Transaction Parameter 509-509 is enabled. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues with custom behavior in section 6.1.2. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, then section 6.1.2 display behavior is disabled . Process Change/Addition Replenishment Tasks As part of EX35, WMOS is enhanced to generate replenishments tasks as C trl+D functionality is invoked. Upon a user shorting a location to 0, regardless of the location going from positive on hand inventory or negative on hand inventory , a replenishment task will be generated. EX35 ensures that there are currently no replenishment tasks in released/in progress/Locked/Disabled status before generating an allocation for a new replenishment task. Upon WMOS validating no replenishment tasks are out there. WMOS utilizes EX35 and creates a replenishment based on the parameters at the location of the picking task. EX35 pulls, location information, item information, Inventory need type and required quantity needed based on the WM allocated quantity at the location. Task creation is generated with priority ‘9’ as configured in C-E28 code id 01 byte 2- 5. . DB Trigger to update negative on-hand to 0 As part of EX35, a DB trigger MCLN_TUB_WMI_NEGATIVE is used to update any location within a pick determination zone whose on-hand quantity is negative, from the negative quantity to 0. Additionally , the trigger sends positive inventory adjustment PIXs for cycle counts to the host for the quantity that was adjusted. Enablement The only enablement for the BD trigger is if the code ID within system code B-331 (Pick locations determination zones) has the parameter 96-96 set to Y. If the 96-96 parameter is set to N then the MCLN_TUB_WMI_NEGATIVE DB trigger is not ran for the locations with that pick determination zone. Validations The DB trigger finds any locations which have the pick determination zones with param 96-96 set to Y and a negative on-hand quantity. Updates The on hand quantity for the locations mentioned in the above validation will have the on-hand quantity set to 0. Outputs There are no custom outputs as part of this extension. Activity Tracking There are no changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX Whenever WM adjusts the on-hand quantity to 0 from the DB trigger, WM sends a n positive inventory adjustment PIX either 300-04-14 or 300-09-14 for active or case pick locations respectively to the host with reason code 01 and a quantity equal to the adjusted quantity. Current state, to adjust inventory of a pick location, a cycle counter must count and book the count. To expedite this process, McLane has approved picking teammates to make an immediate adjustment to the inventory with a user short during picking. For this reason, WMOS is customized to create an inventory adjustment and an associated PIX that is triggered when a floor user executes a user short. With this change, the next chase wave that is run, which could be run immediately after the short, create s a replenishment there is inventory available to replenish the shorted location. Additionally, once inventory reaches zero and or shorted using ctrl-d with negative on e hand quantity, EX28 Replenishment Task Release and Priority Bumping is able to release and/or bump replenishments to reach the location quicker. To raise the chances that the user is shorting the correct Pick Location, WMOS is customized to prompt for a Location Confirmation Scan after the user initiates short in a full short scenario. McLane is also wanting locations which have been driven to negative on-hand inventory by completion of a pick (without shorting) to be adjusted up to have an on-hand qty of 0. The main reason is to not have over replenishments created for locations due to the negative inventory. Currently this is happening since the replenishments take into account the negative quantity. McLane chooses which location this is to be applied to. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Pack Cubed Directed – N ew Update Functionality to Zero Out Location Inventory if a User Shorts a Pick Reporting N Other N Mc Lane picks RF Full Case Picking and RF Active Picking allows the users to short during picking. This short generates a Picking Short Item record which can be chased. Since McLane is constantly running chase throughout the day, it ensures that replenishment can be created at the next chase opportunity if the inventory is immediately adjuste d at the time of the short. For this reason, RF Pack Cubed Directed transaction is modified to automatically adjust the on-hand inventory of the Pick Location to 0 if the user has shorted a pick. Additionally, WMOS sends a PIX to the host system alerting the change in inventory levels. There will be no changes to base cycle count generation or behavior as part of this extension.

Additionally, RF Pack Cubed Directed and RF Pack Pick Cart are customized to prompt for a Location Scan to confirm the Location in a full short scenario. For the locations which have been driven to negative on-hand inventory by completion of a pick (without shorting) a DB trigger MCLN_TUB_WMI_NEGATIVE is used to set these locations to a on-hand qty of 0. The base system code B-331 is leveraged to indicate which pick location determination zones this trigger is applied to. Assumptions This transaction modifies shorting updates in two transactions: RF Pack Cubed Directed RF Pack Pick Cart Transactions are either enabled for this behavior or disabled for this behavior and that is the only check . EX35 sends a PIX to host notifying the inventory adjustment with whichever reason code is configured in the custom system code. There are no changes to base generation and execution of Cycle Counts in this extension. There are no changes to base short warning messages as part of this extension. This extension does not change base allocation trigger points. Updating the on-hand inventory will not create a replenishment allocation. Existing pick allocations from the shorted location will be unchanged. EX35 is not utilized with Cigarette Picking. EX35 ensures that there are currently no replenishment tasks in released/in progress/Locked/Disabled status before generating an allocation for a new replenishment task. Process Change/Addition Replenishment Tasks As part of EX35, WMOS is enhanced to generate replenishments tasks as C trl+D functionality is invoked. Upon a user shorting a location to 0, regardless of the location going from positive on hand inventory or negative on hand inventory , a replenishment task will be generated. EX35 ensures that there are currently no replenishment tasks in released/in progress/Locked/Disabled status before generating an allocation for a new replenishment task. Upon WMOS validating no replenishment tasks are out there. WMOS utilizes EX35 and creates a replenishment based on the parameters at the location of the picking task. EX35 pulls, location information, item information, Inventory need type and required quantity needed based on the WM allocated quantity at the location. Task creation is generated with priority ‘9’ as configured in C-E28 code id 01 byte 2- 5. . Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX35 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX35 is turned off, and no custom EX35 processing will occur. Lastly, RF Pack Pick Cart Transaction Parameter 50 5 -50 5 must be enabled. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues with custom replenishment logic . Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension control for EX35 is turned off, and no custom EX35 replenishment logic will occur.

DB Trigger to update negative on-hand to 0 As part of EX35, a DB trigger MCLN_TUB_WMI_NEGATIVE is used to update any location within a pick determination zone whose on-hand quantity is negative, from the negative quantity to 0. Additionally , the trigger sends positive inventory adjustment PIXs for cycle counts to the host for the quantity that was adjusted. Enablement The only enablement for the BD trigger is if the code ID within system code B-331 (Pick locations determination zones) has the parameter 96-96 set to Y. If the 96-96 parameter is set to N then the MCLN_TUB_WMI_NEGATIVE DB trigger is not ran for the locations with that pick determination zone. Validations The DB trigger finds any locations which have the pick determination zones with param 96-96 set to Y and a negative on-hand quantity. Updates The on hand quantity for the locations mentioned in the above validation will have the on-hand quantity set to 0. Outputs There are no custom outputs as part of this extension. Activity Tracking There are no changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX Whenever WM adjusts the on-hand quantity to 0 from the DB trigger, WM sends a n positive inventory adjustment PIX either 300-04-14 or 300-09-14 for active or case pick locations respectively to the host with reason code 01 and a quantity equal to the adjusted quantity. LM Hooks There are no changes to base labor hooks as part of this extension. Negative Picking As part of EX35 change request , the above section 7.2 logic is no longer active (DB trigger is removed) and instead below listed transactions are enhanced to adjust the on-hand quantity of the location to 0 whenever a user over picks the location and drives the on-hand quantity negative .
